description: Immature sparrow in the company of ~50 WCSP and SOSP. Large Zonotrichia sparrow, faint but distinct yellow patch on forehead. Otherwise fairly drab underparts and and face. White wing bars. Lack of black borders to yellow crown patch, and pale gray, slightly bicolored bill (not bright orange/pink, nor solid, cold gray like WTSP) identified it as an immature bird. Flushed with other sparrows from a field of tall weeds (5-7 feet high) and perched in a tree at the edge of the field about 25 feet away. Less flighty that WCSPs, remained perched for nearly 5 minutes while I observed and referred to Sibley Guide to Birds, mobile version, to confirm age
